text.title: contextual-inquiry Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for contextual-inquiry as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs paraphrase quotes silently when summarizing transcripts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock the tagging agent to verbatim mode: any string in the output not present verbatim in the source transcript is a hallucination. Reject paraphrases for the quote field.

failure-mode.description: Privacy: observation sessions capture bystanders, screen contents, and dictation not belonging to the participant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Redact non-participant PII using a tool like presidio before any LLM ingestion. Remote CI screen recordings often include private windows — pre-trim before sharing with any third-party tool.

failure-mode.description: Agent ranks participant competence, labeling "wrong" workflows as user error.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The finding is always that the system does not fit real work. An agent that judges the workflow as incorrect misses the most valuable signal: workarounds reveal system design failures.

failure-mode.description: Never substitute an LLM-simulated user for a real participant ("synthetic CI").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs have no sensory access to real workplaces. Any "observation" they produce is extrapolation from training data, not from the actual environment being studied.
